observations.recommendation: Management submits a written justification for the non-usage of the above-listed projects. It was also recommended that the above-listed projects be utilized fully and enable the constituents complete enjoyment of the derived benefits from these projects.
observations.reason: The National Housing Authority stopped financing the housing project it originally committed to the LGU. The LGU is still looking for ways in order to complete the project. As to the completed houses, management will deliberate on the selection of deserving beneficiaries

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant sends a written demand to the individuals with existing unliquidated cash advances for settlement/liquidation and issue certifications on accounts with no available documents in accordance with COA Memo 2017-010.
observations.reason: Management continued to send Demand Letters, however, due to the separation of the concerned personnel, they found it difficult to collect the unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the
correct balances of the Accounts Payable and expense accounts.

We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the concerned
offices to promptly submit complete disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting Office for timely
recognition of expenses.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accounting Office to thoroughly review account conversions to ensure
that all transactions are recorded in their appropriate accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accounting Office to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the
classification of the completed infrastructure assets.

We further recommended and Municipal Accountant Office agreed to
ensure the prompt reclassification of completed infrastructure assets to
their respective asset accounts and that corresponding depreciation is
duly recognized.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accounting Office to:

a. Ensure that cash flow information reconciles with the Statement of
Financial Position and Statement of Receipts Sources;

b. Classify cash flows in accordance with IPSAS 2 or disclose any
deviations in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and

c. Present a reconciliation of Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities
with the Net Income in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to:

a. Adjust the accounting records by making the necessary adjusting
entries to correct abnormal balances and accurately report the Real
Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable
accounts;

b. Prepare detailed subsidiary ledgers for these accounts, including
individual taxpayer balances for validation; and

c. Reconcile the accounting book balances with the Real Property Tax
Account Register.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Review and analyze the records of the accounts with abnormal
balances and reconcile accordingly to arrive at the correct balances;

b. Prepare and post adjusting entries to eliminate the abnormal balances;
and

c. Conduct proper and regular monitoring and review of all accounts to
promptly detect abnormal balances and ensure that only accurate
account balances are reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to prepare necessary adjusting entries to derecognize
assets costing below ₱50,000.00.

We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Inventory
Committee to conduct complete physical count of assets and prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Report
on the Physical Count of Road Network System.

We further recommended and Management agreed to require the
Municipal Accounting Office, Inventory Committee and General Services
Office to reconcile their respective records to ensure accurate and reliable
asset reporting.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accounting Office to:

a. Reconcile the inventory ledger cards with the Report of the Physical
Count of Inventories and prepare the necessary adjusting journal
entries to correct any discrepancies; and

b. Strictly implement the perpetual inventory method to ensure timely
recording of inventory transactions, facilitate regular reconciliation,
and support the accurate presentation of inventory balances in the
financial statements.

We also recommended and Management agreed to require the General
Services Office to update the stock cards and ensure that these are
periodically reconciled with the records maintained by the Municipal
Accounting Office to maintain accurate inventory tracking and strengthen
controls over supplies and materials.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Accounting Office to ensure that payments for infrastructure projects are
supported by materials test results in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Engineering Office to ensure that contractors subject the materials used
in the infrastructure projects to the minimum test required for such
materials by engaging the services of accredited laboratories.

We further recommended and Management agreed to adopt a policy
institutionalizing materials testing and setting parameters on which items
of work should be tested.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, with the assistance of
the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office to:

a. Strictly supervise the implementation of the “Construction of
Evacuation and Training Center” to ensure its timely completion;

b. Follow-up on release of the counterpart funding for the two
rehabilitation projects to expedite their implementation;

c. Schedule procurement of long duration infrastructure projects within
the first semester or undertake early procurement activities;

d. Consider phased implementation of major infrastructure projects,
ensuring that each phase is usable and structurally sound; and

e. Ensure that proposed disaster management activities are supported by
sufficient appropriations to prevent idle funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction and Development Council, with the assistance of
the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Development Office, to:

a. Promptly identify all the projects to be charge against the Special Trust
Fund once it becomes available;

b. Program the available balance of ₱5,260.56 to disaster related
activities; and

c. Maintain a single, consolidated LDRRMF Investment Plan that includes
the unexpended LDRRMF from previous years to facilitate easier and
effective monitoring of disaster management activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal
Development Council with the assistance of the Municipal Planning and
Development Office and Bids and Awards Committee to:

a. Reappropriate the ₱4,200,000.00 available 20% Development Fund to
other priority development projects after the reversion;

b. Ensure that development projects are procurement-ready by requiring
the concerned officer to issue a certification that the detailed
engineering activities were conducted and adequate for the
procurement at hand;

c. Regularly assess the projects’ viability to immediately address
bottlenecks encountered; and

d. Fast-track the completion of the detailed engineering activities for the
five development projects amounting to ₱8,400,000.00.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Gender and
Development Focal Point System, in consultation with relevant
stakeholders, to prepare the GAD Agenda for the period 2025 to 2028,
which shall serve as the basis in the formulation of the Gender and
Development Plan and Budget.

We recommended and Management agreed to require:

a. All concerned offices to promptly submit complete disbursement
vouchers and supporting documents to the Municipal Accounting
Office prior to scheduled tax remittances;

b. The Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasury Office to
expedite reconciliation of their records on taxes withheld; and

c. The Municipal Treasury Office to ensure that withheld taxes are
remitted intact and on time.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Human
Resource Management Office and the Municipal Accounting Office
regularly reconcile their records and promptly update the GSIS on any
changes in employee contributions.

We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure that GSIS
contributions are remitted in full and within the prescribed period, as
required by Section 6(b) of RA No. 8291.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned to
comply with laws, rules and regulations to avoid audit suspensions,
disallowances, and charges and settle the same within the prescribed
period to prevent their accumulation.

1.10.11 We recommended that the Head of Agency file a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to the COA, attached with the necessary documents prescribed in COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that management submit the lacking pensioners’ ID to COA for verification.  We further recommend that the claimants reimburse the excess pension.  Lastly, it is also recommended that the LGU adhere with DSWD MC No. 16-21 and the IRR of RA 11916 in the granting and distribution of social pension to senior citizens.
We recommended that collectors, who have yet to apply for bond shall not be allowed to perform collection functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their Cash in Bank Balances to adhere with the provisions of Section 181-c of COA Circular No. 368-91 dated December 19, 1991 and the provisions of Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards.
We recommended that the LGU stop the practice of granting honorarium to officers of the Provincial Legal Office on top of their monthly salary.

We recommended that the LGU submit the written conformity and acquiescence of the Solicitor General or the Government Corporate Counsel and the written concurrence of the Commission on Audit for hiring a private lawyer.

We recommended that management submit the complete distribution list to the COA for review and verification. It is also recommended that management through the MDRRMO, plan ahead the purchase of supplies especially food items for distribution, in order to preserve its quality and avoid wastage of government funds.

We recommended that, henceforth, the Local School Board (LSB) secure received transmittal letters or file copies of the submitted quarterly SEF utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office, through the DepEd-Division of Bohol, copy furnished the Sangguniang Bayan, DBM and DILG Regional Offices, for verification purposes. Moreover, we recommend that the LSB comply with the provisions of Section 6.1 of the aforementioned joint circular to ensure proper accountability and transparency in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

The Municipal Accountant submits a written justification for the deficiency noted. It was also recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recognizing expenses only when paid and not when incurred.

Management takes the following courses of action:

a.	Verify the dormant bank accounts and determine the purposes for opening the same by securing copies of the authority from the Local Sanggunian.  

b.	If the purpose is determined to be already served/completed, an authority from the Sanggunian and approval from the Chief Executive be secured to transfer the funds to the Municipality’s main depository accounts;

c.	Trust funds received for purposes which could no longer be pursued or are no longer needed by the recipient agency, shall be returned to the source agencies.

Management submits a written justification for the non-implementation of 38% of its GAD Funds.  Moreover, it was also recommended that management take into consideration the provisions of RA 9710 and other GAD related laws and issuances in crafting projects, activities and programs financed by GAD funds so that, the appropriation will only be utilized for the purpose for which it is intended.

Management prioritizes and considers filling-up of the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.

Further, personnel movement has been effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions.

Management submits a written justification for not submitting the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Trip Tickets. It was also recommended that management adhere with Sections B and D of Circular No. 77-61 in the liquidation of their monthly fuel consumption.
The Head of the Agency ensures that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions have to be enforced.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to send a written demand to the individuals with existing unliquidated cash advances for settlement/liquidation and issue certifications on accounts with no available documents in accordance with COA Memo 2017-010.
We recommended that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions have to be enforced.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit a written justification for the deficiency noted. It was also recommended that the Municipal Accountant stop the practice of recognizing expenses only when paid and not when incurred.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Verify the dormant bank accounts and determine the purposes for opening the same by securing copies of the authority from the Local Sanggunian.
If the purpose is determined to be already served/completed, an authority from the Sanggunian and approval from the Chief Executive be secured to transfer the funds to the Municipality’s main depository accounts;
Trust funds received for purposes which could no longer be pursued or are no longer needed by the recipient agency, shall be returned to the source agencies.

We recommended that management submit a written justification for the non-implementation of 38% of its GAD Funds.  Moreover, it was also recommended that management take into consideration the provisions of RA 9710 and other GAD related laws and issuances in crafting projects, activities and programs financed by GAD funds so that, the appropriation will only be utilized for the purpose for which it is intended.

We recommended that management submit a written justification for the non-usage of the above-listed projects. It was also recommended that the above-listed projects be utilized fully and enable the constituents complete enjoyment of the derived benefits from these projects.

We recommended that management prioritize and consider filling-up of the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.

Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

We recommended that management submit a written justification for not submitting the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Trip Tickets. It was also recommended that management adhere with Sections B and D of Circular No. 77-61 in the liquidation of their monthly fuel consumption.

We recommended that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the source agencies.  Remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and will be used for other projects of the LGU.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor the vouchers and supporting documents of the recorded accounts payable.  We also recommend, henceforth, that management ensure that obligations included as accounts payable are properly supported with complete documentation to substantiate the existence of valid payables.
We recommended that management require the contractor to make a full depth repair/reconstruction on the damaged blocks, otherwise, this will be deducted from the contract cost.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct concerned municipal officials look into the issues that hinders the inability of the local government unit to fully implement the projects and execute strategies to prioritize the implementation of the 20% developmental projects.

We recommended that Management conduct timely general or particular revision of real property assessments per Section 219 of R.A. 7160 and Section I, Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations so as to enable collection of additional real property taxes which could be derived from the revised real property assessments.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the source agencies.  Remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and will be used for other projects of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables with that of the Deferred Credits accounts and effect adjustments, if any, in order to reflect the accurate balances of these accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Agriculturist regularly monitor the changes and movement of Biological Assets to facilitate the proper valuation of these assets in order to reflect an accurate value of the account.  Likewise, conduct a physical count of these assets and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Inventories/Biological assets.
We recommend that management requires its officials and employees to support claim for taxi fare and travel expenses with Reimbursement Expenses Receipt as required upon COA Circular 2021-1.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies.  Remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and will be used for other projects of the agency.
observations.managementAction: All projects were completed and the remaining amount of P252,679.79 out of P25,770,112.24 are for return to source agency.  Due to LGU has yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant to send a written demand to the individuals with existing unliquidated cash advances for settlement/liquidation and issue certifications on accounts with no available documents in accordance with COA Memo 2017-010.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent and a balance of P49,223.16 remain as of December 31, 2021.  Collection process is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions have to be enforced.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to concern employees and former employees of the municipality.  Out of the balance amounting to P99,005.65, P12,005.65 were collected as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has utilized the Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) of 2015 for its GAD database pending the completion of the Updated CBMS which is currently on-going.  It was suggested in the JMC-2013-01 that CBMS can be used in enriching and developing a Gender-responsive Ecological Profile and database.  The LGU also created a GAD Focal Point System to help identify gender issues and concerns to be brought up during planning, programming and policy formulation of the agency using CBMS as the database.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has utilized the Community Based Monitoring System (CBMS) of 2015 for its GAD database pending the completion of the Updated CBMS which is currently on-going.  It was suggested in the JMC-2013-01 that CBMS can be used in enriching and developing a Gender-responsive Ecological Profile and database.  The LGU also created a GAD Focal Point System to help identify gender issues and concerns to be brought up during planning, programming and policy formulation of the agency using CBMS as the database.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors.  Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise, if proved futile
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were continually delivered to the concerned debtors, but no collections were recovered yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly deposit all collections intact on a daily basis or not later than the next banking day and desist from the practice of accumulating huge amount of cash balances in her custody pursuant to Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) install internal control over the handling of collections and deposits by the Municipal Treasurer in order to safeguard assets and promote efficient operations in consonance with the fiscal responsibility of the Agency Head provided by Section 124 of PD No. 1445; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor at the end of each month the mandatory remittance of collections by the Municipal Treasurer in consonance with the Accountant’s responsibility to install and maintain an internal audit system in the local government unit pursuant to Section 474(b) of RA No. 7160; (c) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) adjust the cashbook balance for the reconciling items noted in the Reconciliation Statement of Accountability; and to (ii) check the correctness of individual Report of Collections and Deposits (RCDs) against corresponding official receipts before accepting collections from the liquidating officer and the individual collectors; and (d) require the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (i) record the transactions for the Cash Local Treasury account in a timely manner; and (ii) perform reconciliation of accounting and treasury records on a monthly basis.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the MA to present the Cash in Bank – LCTD in the Investment account; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) verify and communicate with the bank as to the sources of the unrecorded/unaccounted deposits totaling to ₱421,016.51 for proper recognition in the books; (ii) cause the immediate submission of all paid DVs and supporting documents totaling ₱17,993,787.73 to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; (iii) exercise due diligence in the performance of  official duties and responsibilities by desisting from the practice of entrusting paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to unauthorized persons; (c) require the Municipal Accountant to record the unrecorded/unaccounted deposits totaling to ₱421,016.51  into a temporary trust liability account which shall be adjusted  in the books  based on the result of the Municipal Treasurer’s verification with the bank;  (d) re-activate or close the dormant PVB account to avoid incurrence of dormancy charges.
In addition, we recommended that the LCE provide timeline for the submission of the said DVs, otherwise, require the suspension of payment of the salaries of the MT and other concerned officials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require  (a)  the Municipal Accountant to (i) reclassify  the Small Value Items in the PPE accounts as Inventories upon purchase and recognize them as an expense upon issuance to the end-user; (ii)the Municipal Engineer  to assess the present value of the damaged constructed overflow bridge at Sitio Abo, Pulong Sampaloc and to report to the Municipal Accountant  its   impaired   condition for proper disclosure and/or  derecognition in the books pursuant to the provisions of International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 21 or the Impairment of Non-Cash Generating Assets; and (iii) the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the procurement of solar lights amounting to ₱5,241,502.00 under the PPE, Power Supply Systems account and to recognize  the asset  under  the Construction Materials Inventory account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) designate an officer/employee  to be fully in-charge in  the receipt, issuance, monitoring of inventory items, and the preparation and submission of the required reports pursuant to  existing regulations; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer, being  the property and supply officer,  to reconcile the balance of the inventory accounts per books and to submit the necessary RIS and RSMI to reflect the accurate balance of the account; (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the erroneous recording of the accounts pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-004 dated January 31, 2020; (d) require the Municipal Engineer to immediately program and provide timelines for the installation of the solar lights for the benefit of the constituents; (e) limit the procurement of solar lights and other supplies and materials  to what is immediate and necessary to avoid wastage of government funds;  (f) construct an adequate storeroom/bodega to properly store  supplies and materials and protect the assets from the danger of pilferage, damage, and unnecessary loss; and (g)  limit access thereof to authorized personnel only.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies.  Remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency and will be used for other projects of the agency.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to send a written demand to the individuals with existing unliquidated cash advances for settlement/liquidation and issue certifications on accounts with no available documents in accordance with COA Memo 2017-010.
We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions have to be enforced.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities that are not yet fully implemented before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the Municipal Treasurer to (i) immediately submit all  paid DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; (ii) exercise due diligence in the performance of her official duties and responsibilities as an accountable officer by desisting from the practice of entrusting paid disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to unauthorized persons; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement vouchers  to the Audit Team for verification and audit, and  record the deposits into                          a temporary trust liability account which shall be adjusted  in the books  based on the result of the Municipal Treasurer’s verification with the LBP;   (c) cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the Municipal Treasurer and  other officials concerned relative to the 33  unsubmitted DVs of  current and prior years, until they shall  have complied with the complete submission thereof  pursuant to Section 122.2 of PD No. 1445; and (d) impose criminal sanction/administrative disciplinary action against the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned public officials for repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements of  laws and regulations on the submission of DVs.

We     recommended       that        the 
Municipal Accountant (a) identify all the items which were incorrectly recognized as PPE in the books; (b) prepare the adjusting entries on the affected asset and equity accounts; and (c) recognize the appropriate Accumulated Depreciation based on the estimated useful life and recoverable value of the constructed road network.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the development of the 2019 AM road projects before the fund expires on December 31, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) direct the MPDO and the Municipal Engineer to expedite and prioritize the implementation of projects under the 20% DF; (b) strictly observe the guidelines in project information dissemination as stated in COA Circular No. 2013-04 dated January 30, 2013.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure in the preparation of the next annual budget that the  allocation of the repairs and maintenance of Repairs and Maintenance of Buildings, Other Structures and Infrastructure Assets  shall be included in  the budget for Repairs and Maintenance of the  Municipality for  General Fund instead of the LDRRMF; (b) expedite the disbursement of funds for the succeeding years; and (c) purchase the necessary disaster  supplies and equipment  consistent with the Minimum Standards for Disaster Risk Reduction and Management to help in  preparedness and mitigation of disaster hazards.

We recommended that the Municipality continuously abide by the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Waste Management Act of 2000 to sustain the best environmental management practices and gain credence as a model of environmental sustainability.

We recommended that the LCE require the MSWD Officer to continue to adhere to the pertinent provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016 re: Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women on the responsiveness and proper attribution of the Municipality’s GAD-related programs and activities.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to remit all tax dues regularly within the prescribed time frame.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with Section 6 of RA No. 8291 on the withholding and remittance of GSIS contributions and loan amortizations.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with Sections 2 and 3 of RA No. 9679 on the withholding and remittance of Pag-IBIG contributions and loan amortizations.
We recommended that Management continue to faithfully comply with Section 20 of the Revised IRR of RA 7875 on the withholding and remittance of PhilHealth contributions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and (b) require the immediate settlement of disallowed claims.
We recommend that management prepare Annual Procurement Program based on the approved format provided by the GPPB, in accordance with Section 373 or RA 9160, Article II Section 7 of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: Prepared the required Annual Procurement Plan prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC should minimize the practice of procuring goods and services by “shopping” or “small value procurement”. Bidding should be the primary mode as mentioned in RA 9184. The BAC secretary should prepare the appropriate Annual Procurement Plan to guide the agency in the procurement of goods and services. So that the use of shopping or small value procurement as mode of procurement could be minimized.
observations.managementAction: Prepared a detailed Annual Procurement Plan in the prescribed format.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the lacking documents to the Commission on Audit (COA).  Moreover, management must prepare its liquidation reports and related documentation in accordance with section 4(6) of PD 1445 to ensure transparency in all transactions.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management, through BAC, submit a written explanation why the regulations for the procurement of consultancy contract involving individual consultants were not observed.
observations.managementAction: Written explanation sent.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions have to be enforced.
observations.managementAction: The agency will file a request for write off since ₱379,361.78 of the total receivables for disallowance are those has been long outstanding and cannot be identified amidst the effort of the accountant. The remaining balance will be enforced on CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of liquidation reports to the concerned  source agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds  after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency that can be used for other agency’s projects.
observations.managementAction: Implementation/Utilization of the projects is on-going. Most of the fund transfers from the national agencies are just being downloaded on the last of quarter of CY 2019. That is why the agency has difficulties in the implementation before the year end. However, the programs in which the fund transfers are for will be implemented in CY 2020. It will be utilized, and the unexpended balance will be returned.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors. Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise, if proved futile, request for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were continually delivered to the concerned debtors, but no collections were recovered as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE (a) instruct the officials involved in the processing of claims to be more diligent in evaluating supporting papers of claims pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid suspensions or disallowances in audit; and (b) require the immediate refund of disallowed claims once these have become final and executory.

1.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require the Municipal
Treasurer to (i) immediately submit all paid DVs and supporting documents to the
Accounting Office for recording in the books; (ii) exercise due diligence in the
performance of her official duties and responsibilities as an accountable officer by desisting from the practice of entrusting paid disbursement vouchers and supporting
documents to unauthorized persons; (b) require the Municipal Accountant to submit
the disbursement vouchers  to the Audit Team for verification and audit, and record
the deposits into a temporary trust liability account which shall be adjusted in the
books based on the result of the Municipal Treasurer’s verification with the LBP;
(c) cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the Municipal Treasurer and
other officials concerned relative to the 33 unsubmitted DVs of current and prior
years, until they shall have complied with the complete submission thereof pursuant
to Section 122.2 of PD No. 1445; and (d) impose criminal sanction/administrative
disciplinary action against the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned public
officials for repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements of laws and
regulations on the submission of DVs.

2.10. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) identify all the items
which were incorrectly recognized as PPE in the books; (b) prepare the adjusting
entries on the affected asset and equity accounts; and (c) recognize the appropriate
Accumulated Depreciation based on the estimated useful life and recoverable value
of the constructed road network.
We recommend that management prepare Annual Procurement Program based on the approved format provided by the GPPB, in accordance with Section 373 or RA 9160, Article II Section 7 of RA 9184.

We recommend that the BAC should minimize the practice of procuring goods and services by “shopping” or “small value procurement”. Bidding should be the primary mode as mentioned in RA 9184. The BAC secretary should prepare the appropriate Annual Procurement Plan to guide the agency in the procurement of goods and services. So that the use of shopping or small value procurement as mode of procurement could be minimized.

We recommend that management effect the immediate implementation of fund transfers/grants and the submission of  liquidation reports to the concerned  source agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds  after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency that can be used for other agency’s projects.
We recommend that the Head of the Agency ensure that settlement of the disallowances/charges is made, otherwise, appropriate actions has to be enforced.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents to the Commission on Audit (COA).  Moreover, management must prepare its liquidation reports and related documentation in accordance with section 4(6) of PD 1445 to ensure transparency in all transactions.
We recommend that Management, through BAC, submit a written explanation why the regulations for the procurement of consultancy contract involving individual consultants were not observed.

We recommended that the LCE: (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to reevaluate reconciling items in the submitted Statements of Bank Reconciliation and carefully analyze/adjust future bank reconciliation statements with prudence; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to request for bank snapshots of the account history inclusive of the unrecorded bank deposits and to submit the same to the Accounting Office for immediate recording and to the COA Auditor’s Office for verification and audit; (c) require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit all CY 2018 unsubmitted paid DVs and supporting documents to the Accounting Office for recording in the books; and require the Municipal Accountant to submit the same  to the COA Auditor’s Office for verification and audit; (d) cause the suspension of payment of the salaries of the officials concerned relative to the 16 unsubmitted DVs of  prior years, until they have complied with the complete submission, pursuant to Section 122.2 of PD No. 1445; (e) impose sanction/ administrative disciplinary action against concerned public officials for repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements of the aforementioned law on the submission of DVs, which omission
constitute neglect of duty, pursuant to Section 127 of PD No.1445; and (f) instruct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the 
balance of PNB Checking Account No. 113-003-003 after verifying the account status.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustment on the liquidated Confidential and Intelligence Fund aggregating to ₱1,650,000.00 pursuant to Joint Circular COA/DBM/DILG/GCG/DND Joint Circular (JC) No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the (a) Property Office to prepare/maintain Property Cards for PPE and reconcile their records with the Accounting Office in order to determine the accuracy of the account balance as of year-end; and (b) Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory-taking yearly to determine the actual existence and condition of the PPE.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned Department Heads to continue to adhere to the policy guidelines and procedures in the implementation of fund transfers from National Government Agencies provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001 issued June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineer to (a) prioritize the implementation of projects under the 20% EDF; (b) compel the contractor to rectify the particular project stated herein which was unduly damaged in less than six months; and (c) ardently comply to the promulgations of the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101.

We recommended that the LCE continue to satisfactorily adhere to the guidelines set forth in RA No. 10121 regarding the planning, budgeting and implementation of the 5% Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund.

We recommended that the LCE, thru the Local School Board (a) revisit the SEF-related provisions of the Local Government Code and Joint Circular 2017-01 of DepEd, DBM and DILG and strictly comply thereafter to avoid any further misallocation of funds to PPAs unrelated to the SEF mandates; and (b) exercise prudence in the disbursement of SEF funds by strictly following Paragraph 1, Section 4, of PD No. 1445 or the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO to continuously abide with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003 or the Ecological Waste Management Act of 2000 to sustain its best environmental management practices and gain credence as a model of environmental sustainability.

We recommended that LCE require the concerned departments to strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of the Local Government Code, Magna Carta for Women and COA Memorandum Circular No. 2012-003 on the planning, budgeting and implementation of gender and development projects, programs and activities.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to continuously comply with the full remittance of the balance of the Due to BIR account within the prescribed time frame.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to continue to adhere to GSIS laws, rules and regulations on the deduction of premiums and loan payments and the remittance thereof together with the government share within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to continue the satisfactory performance by adhering to the provisions of Pag-IBIG laws, rules and regulations on the proper withholding of monthly premium contributions and loan amortizations, and their remittance thereafter.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to continue to adhere to the provisions of PhilHealth laws, rules and regulations on the proper withholding of monthly premium contributions and the remittance thereof.
We recommend the municipal accountant to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) for the dropping of this account and make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Respective records of OMA and GSO updated and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to discontinue the monthly deductions for loan repayments on personal loans secured by the municipal officials and employees from private lending institutions by not renewing the contract agreements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 48 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10964.
observations.managementAction:
Last deduction: CBB-January 10, 2019 CFI-July 18, 2019
As for FCB, the LGU-Trinidad has an existing MOA with FCB on the LGU-Trinidad availment of FCB’s payroll servicing thru Pitakard. Under paragraph 25 of the said MOA, the LGU has the responsibility for direct payroll deduction. A copy of MOA is attached herewith and marked as Annex C-1. Also attached is a copy of FCB’s accreditation from BSP Marked as Annex C-2.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to comply with the requirements set in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01 in the budget and utilization of its 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Although as per AIP, the incentives for MESWM’s Best Practices in under MOOE, during the actual implementation, it is actually utilized as Capital Outlay based on the Program of Works submitted by the winning barangays. But in order to erase confusion or doubt as to whether it is MOOE or CO, these projects/programs are already allocated and charged to GF proper instead of 20% DF starting CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to be prudent in the disbursement of scarce resources of the local government, thereby, finding an effective and efficient solution on addressing the duplication of their internet expenses in consonance to Sec. 3.2 of COA Circular 85-55A, dated September 8, 1985.
observations.managementAction: The reason why the MO has a separate subscription is that it is also used by the BAC Secretariat in the posting of Bids which we can’t afford to have delays caused by slow internet connection. The SB has also equally important data to relay via internet which delay could have legal implications. We are praying to your good office to be understanding of us in this particular situation. For 2020, our internet subscription with Innove will be allocated to TMC to address the needs of those school especially in the computer laboratories and in order to avoid this seeming duplication.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management have to see to it that all issuances of tangible properties to end-users are supported with PAR as prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386.
observations.managementAction: A copy of DV with PAR is attached and shown to the auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to submit a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products procured fall within and conformed with PNDF, Volume I, current edition, pursuant to the above mentioned EO and AO, otherwise the transaction will be disallowed in audit. Henceforth, all procurement of drugs and medicines need to be accompanied with this certification.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the DVV with MHO certification is attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management and concerned officials to comply with documentary requirements as required in Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 relative to repairs and maintenance of aircraft, equipment and motor vehicles for evaluation and verification purposes and to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post audit.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the DV with repair history of PPE is attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU administrator, treasury and accounting offices to adhere to COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly account and control gasoline, oil and lubricants consumption and use of motor vehicles. Moreover, we recommend that the LGU submit the monthly fuel consumption reports, monthly report official travels and properly filled out trip tickets to the auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
observations.managementAction: Copies of the Monthly Fuel Consumption Report and Monthly Report of Official Travels were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to strictly comply the documentary requirements provided by COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the granting of cash advance to local travel.
observations.managementAction: A copy of the DV with a stamp/note signifying that the Payee has no unliquidated cash advanced is attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management plan their projects efficiently in order to avoid delays and fast track project implementation.
observations.managementAction: A photo has been submitted to COA

The GAD Focal Point System conducted its quarterly meeting. On July 23, 2019 and one of its agenda is the report on the status of implementation of GAD Plan and utilization of GAD Budget. In this way, we hope to improve our performance with regard to the implementation of GAD Plan and budget in CY 2019 which will ultimately ensure to the benefit of our constituents.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to direct the Property Officer to process the application for disposal of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE as of the year end.
observations.managementAction: We already submitted our application for our disposal to COA-Bohol and they have also forwarded the request for the scheduling of the disposal. Consequently, on November 11, 2019, we received an Inspection/Appraisal Report as of October 1, 2019 from the technical audit staff interposing no objection to our appraisal of the unserviceable properties. We then conducted two public bidding for the sale of the said unserviceable properties on November 19, 2019 and December 17, 2019. However, no bidders participated during the first and second bidding which led the committee to pass Resolution No. 003-2019 use the Sale thru Negotiation as mode of disposal of the unserviceable properties. As of date, some of the unserviceable properties are already sold.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be affected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing entry of new applicants for lower level positions.
observations.managementAction: Of the 10 vacant positions, 2 are already filled as of July 2019 specifically the Admin Officer I and Municipal Agriculture Officer. Professionals to help better serve our constituents and at the same give opportunities for employees to be promoted or create new job opportunities for those aspiring for work. However, in as much we want to fill up all these positions, we have also a qualification standard to set follow as set by the Civil Service Commission and sadly, we are still waiting for qualified applicants for the remaining unfilled positions.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries’ avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2019. We already reached 76% utilization of the appropriated budget of P21,335,958.00. In spite of our efforts to fully utilize the Development Fund, some factors affect our implementation of 20% DF which are sometimes beyond our control.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management effect the immediate submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Implementation/Utilization of the projects is on-going. Utilized the remaining balances and returned the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors. Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise, if proved futile, request for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were continually delivered to the concerned debtors, but no collections were recovered as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to direct the Property Officer to process the application for disposal of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE as of the year end.
We recommend the municipal accountant to draw a journal entry voucher (JEV) for the dropping of this account and make necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend the management to discontinue the monthly deductions for loan repayments on personal loans secured by the municipal officials and employees from private lending institutions by not renewing the contract agreements. Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of Section 48 of the General Provisions of Republic Act No. 10964.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents. Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby allowing entry of new applicants for lower level positions.
We recommend the management to comply with the requirements set in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-01 in the budget and utilization of its 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that the municipal mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Council (MPDC) to maximize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries’ avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom.

We recommend the management to be prudent in the disbursement of scarce resources of the local government, thereby, finding an effective and efficient solution on addressing the duplication of their internet expenses in consonance to Sec. 3.2 of COA Circular 85-55A, dated September 8, 1985.
We recommend that management have to see to it that all issuances of tangible properties to end-users are supported with PAR as prescribed in COA Circular No. 92-386

We recommend the management to submit a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products procured fall within and conformed with PNDF, Volume I, current edition, pursuant to the above mentioned EO and AO, otherwise the transaction will be disallowed in audit. Henceforth, all procurement of drugs and medicines need to be accompanied with this certification.

We recommend that management and concerned officials to comply with documentary requirements as required in Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 relative to repairs and maintenance of aircraft, equipment and motor vehicles for evaluation and verification purposes and to preclude the suspension or disallowance of transactions during post audit.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the LGU administrator, treasury and accounting offices to adhere to COA Circular 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 to properly account and control gasoline, oil and lubricants consumption and use of motor vehicles. Moreover, we recommend that the LGU submit the monthly fuel consumption reports, monthly report official travels and properly filled out trip tickets to the auditor for verification purposes to determine the reasonableness of fuel consumed during the period.
We recommend management to strictly comply the documentary requirements provided by COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the granting of cash advance to local travel.
We recommend that the management plan their projects efficiently in order to avoid delays and fast track project implementation.

We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.

We recommend that the Accounting and the General Services Sections be directed to update and reconcile their respective records in order to ascertain the correctness and accuracy of valuation of PPE in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Respective records of OMA and GSO updated and reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the accounting office to continue the verification and analysis of the factors contributing the occurrence of the abnormal balances. If appropriate adjustments should be done in the books to effect the necessary corrections, submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and supporting documents to the Commission on Audit.
observations.managementAction: Subsidiary ledgers of Due to Pag-ibig, GSIS and Philhealth were adjusted and corrected.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all financial assistance and all payments be supported with necessary documents.
observations.managementAction: A copy of SB Resolution authorizing LCE to enter into agreement with barangays and MOU were submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Engineer to submit to the COA the documents necessary to justify the Variation Order being implemented on the project: Construction of Evacuation Center at Hinlayagan Ilaya, Trinidad, Bohol.
observations.managementAction: Documents necessary to justify the variation order being implemented on the project: Construction of Evacuation Center at Hinlayagan Ilaya, Trinidad, Bohol was already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the BAC to revisit its procurement process with the end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in every procurement activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184. Further, we recommend that management make their advertisement more known through a wide dissemination of bid opportunities to invite more prospective suppliers to participate in the bidding process and therefore afford a wider range of choices for the municipality and secure a contract most beneficial to it.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is strictly adhering to the provisions of RA 9184 in each procurement activity especially in its advertisement. The number of suppliers participating in the bidding is beyond their control.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of acquiring goods through shopping and adhere to the provisions of Section 10, Article IV of Republic Act 9184, which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government.  Limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Section 52, Rule XVI of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC adheres with the provisions of Sec. 10, Article IV of RA 9184 and used the alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Sec 52, Rule XVI of RA 9184. Supporting documents are properly attached in consonance with
Sec 4(6) of PD 1445 and Sec 17.1, Rule VI of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to refrain from procuring items which are not in accordance with its approved PPMP and APP and strictly abide with the provisions of Sec. 7.2 and 7.4 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations on Republic Act 9184 to uphold adequate and careful procurement planning.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is now procuring items strictly in accordance with its approved APP and PPMP.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management follow proper procurement process on consulting services thereby adhering to the government policy of competition, transparency, economy and efficiency in the procurement process, which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government.  Limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Section 53, Rule XVI of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The LGU now follows proper procurement process on consulting services and limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Sec. 53, Rule XVI of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management effect the immediate submission of liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies and remit the remaining balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to be returned to the source agency.
observations.managementAction: Implementation/Utilization of the projects is on-going. Utilized the remaining balances and returned the unexpended balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to instruct the GSO and the Accountant to comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of drugs & medicines and agricultural &marine suppliers and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct affected accounts. Further, the Municipal Officials have to limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable programs, projects and activities that directly benefit its constituents in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.
observations.managementAction: LGU complied with the provisions of Sec 114 and 121 of LGU NGAS Manual for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of drugs and medicines and agricultural & marine suppliers and set up inventory for said accounts which are expense upon utilization in accordance with DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not and follow the procedure provided in Section 59, NGAS Vol. I cited as follows:

A spoiled or stale check shall be marked cancelled on its face and reported as follows:
Xxx.
For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.

For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and which require placement, a new check may be issued upon submission of the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check. The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be reported chronologically in the RCI.
observations.managementAction: Original DVs and supporting documents returned to the Accountant who subsequently prepared JEVs to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that GAD Focal Point System regularly assess the status of implementation of GAD Plan and Budget to timely and properly evaluate those activities yet for implementation before the calendar year ends, in order to fully maximize GAD funds and provide new or better services to the intended beneficiaries pursuant to the provisions of DBM-NEDA-NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004-1.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is trying its best to monitor the implementation of GAD projects and activities and hopefully we will be able to maximize utilization in CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make representation with DPWH in Ubay, Bohol and the other implementing agencies for the turn-over of the buildings and other records. We also recommend that management disclose the estimated costs of the repair/rehabilitation of the Trinidad Municipal Building in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU made representation with DPWH Ubay who constructed the New Municipal Building to request for a certificate of turnover which will be used in recording the said building in our books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU and PO immediately device a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) to instantly obtain maximum potential benefits of the asset. Further, we recommend that the necessary materials for its operational use be fully installed for it to be ready and fit. Furthermore, recommend that management identify a recipient People’s Organization (PO) for the second unit of Flatbed Dryer so that the asset may be used for its intended beneficiary. Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) involving the two parties, LGU and PO. Meanwhile, the floating tillers were already distributed to the barangays.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors. Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise, if proved futile, request for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were continually delivered to the concerned debtors but no collections were recovered as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the management to require all presently employed municipal officers/employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances by issuing demand letters, otherwise, withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 1445. Further, we recommend that management reissue demand letters to those municipal officials/employees who are no longer connected to the LGU and assign a personnel/team to monitor the status/accomplishment.
observations.managementAction: Sent out new demand letters. This time with return cards.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Property Officer to process the application for disposal of unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD No. 1445. Upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the accurate value of PPE as of the year end.
observations.managementAction: The LGU is preparing and tagging the PPEs subject for disposal. After which, the property officer will process the application for disposal by CY 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU allocate 1% of its IRA for the implementation of LCPC-related programs, projects and activities in accordance with Section 15 of RA 9344. Further, the LGU need to prepare its LCPC Plan based on the applicable PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 annually and ensures that the amount appropriated for LCPC is fully utilized during the year it is appropriated.
observations.managementAction: Included as a separate appropriation in its annual budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Accounting and the General Services Sections be
directed to update and reconcile their respective records in order to ascertain the
correctness and accuracy of valuation of PPE in the financial statements.

We recommend the management to require all presently employed municipal
officers/employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances by issuing
demand letters, otherwise, withhold or suspend salaries of officials and employees
for non-compliance therewith pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89
of PD 1445. Further, we recommend that management reissue demand letters to
those municipal officials/employees who are no longer connected to the LGU and
assign a personnel/team to monitor the status/accomplishment.

We recommend the accounting office to continue the verification and
analysis of the factors contributing the occurrence of the abnormal balances. If
appropriate adjustments should be done in the books to effect the necessary
corrections, submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and supporting documents to
the Commission on Audit.

We recommend that management effect the immediate submission of
liquidation reports to the concerned source agencies and remit the remaining
balances of the funds after the completion of the project, unless otherwise stipulated
in the Memorandum of Agreement that unused funds shall no longer be required to
be returned to the source agency.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to
ascertain whether the checks were claimed or not and follow the procedure
provided in Section 59, NGAS Vol. I cited as follows:
A spoiled or stale check shall be marked cancelled on its face and reported as
follows:
Xxx.
1. For stale checks which have been unclaimed and thus, the original DV
and supporting documents are still with the Treasurer, the cancelled
check shall be presented in the RCI after the last check issued for the
period indicated in the report. The original DV and supporting
documents shall be returned to the Accountant who shall prepare a JEV
to record the transaction as Accounts Payable.
2. For checks which became spoiled or stale in the hands of the payee and
which require placement, a new check may be issued upon submission of
the spoiled or stale check to the Treasurer. A certified copy of the DV
shall be requested from the Auditor for presentation to the
Administrator/Local Chief Executive who shall countersign the check.
The cancelled check shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared
at the period of cancellation. The replacement check shall also be
reported chronologically in the RCI.

We recommend that all financial assistance and all payments be supported
with necessary documents.

We recommend management to instruct the GSO and the Accountant to
comply strictly with the provisions of Sections 114 and 121 of the Manual on NGAS
for the proper recording of purchases and utilization of drugs & medicines and
agricultural &marine suppliers and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
correct affected accounts. Further, the Municipal Officials have to limit the
utilization of the 20% Development Fund to viable and sustainable programs,
projects and activities that directly benefit its constituents in accordance with
DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01.

Direct the Property Officer to process the application for disposal of
unserviceable properties to the COA Auditor supported by the forms pursuant to
the Handbook on Property and Supply Management System, and Section 79 of PD
No. 1445. Upon disposal, drop the cost of the PPE from the books to reflect the
accurate value of PPE as of the year end.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Engineer to submit
to the COA the documents necessary to justify the Variation Order being
implemented on the project: Construction of Evacuation Center at Hinlayagan
Ilaya, Trinidad, Bohol.

We recommend that management, being the source agency, to coordinate with the proper officials of the implementing agency in their subsequent fund transfers to be able to execute a Memorandum of Agreement in pursuance of COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: During the exit conference on February 20, 2017, we come up with an agreement that we will consider materiality in preparing the MOA for fund transfers to the different barangays of the LGU. Hence, for fund transfers amounting to Php10,000.00 above we will execute a MOA between the LGU and the respective barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor the utilization of the LDRRMF. We further recommend the management to submit an explanation for incurring expenses in excess of the amount allowed. Lastly, we recommend that Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF be prepared quarterly.
observations.managementAction: The agency has improved its utilization of LDRRMF. With the regards to the expenses in excess of the amount allowed, management explained that the excess amount refers to the previous year’s balance of the fund. Meanwhile, Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF quarterly was prepared by the accountant but accordingly not submitted to the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Quarterly reports on the Utilization of the LDRRMF were prepared but were still not submitted to the Office of the Auditor (COA).

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management to ensure that expenses charged to the LDRRMF shall be in accordance with the law and COA Circular 2012-002; submit an explanation for items incurred in excess of the amount allowed.
observations.managementAction: With the regards to the expenses in excess of the amount allowed, management explained that the excess amount refers to the previous year’s balance of the fund.
observations.reason: The excess was explained as referring to the previous year’s balance of the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare and submit the required Report on the Utilization of LDRRMF before the 15th day of each month to COA as required under COA circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF quarterly was prepared by the accountant but accordingly not submitted to the Commission on Audit.
observations.reason: Reports were prepared quarterly but not submitted to COA as required.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management plan the Local School Board Budget for Special Education Fund (SEF) ahead of the deadline set in RA 5447.
observations.managementAction: LSB Members were instructed to strictly abide with the provision of RA 5447 by planning and enacting the succeeding SEF budget (CY 2019) in the proper timeline.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Agency Head to strictly observe and comply with the established laws, rules and documentary requirements on the disbursement of public funds, and that management assign an official next-in-rank familiar with payroll and petty cash transactions to facilitate approval of such transactions in lieu of the Agency Head when temporarily absent.
observations.managementAction: Office order No. 58 series of 2016 was executed to Mr Jose N Tabada Jr to be the alternate/substitute disbursing officer for the payroll fund of LGU and attach copy to the cash advance voucher in consonance with Sec. 4(5) & (6) of P.D. 1445.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Agency Head to issue an appointment copy to the cash disbursing officer in order that disbursements may be properly authorized in conformity with COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Office order No. 01 series of 2017 was executed to Mrs. Maria Evelyn E Baradas for her designation as Alternate/Substitute Disbursing Officer for LGU Payrolls. Copy of said office order is attached for your reference.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and record succeeding transactions in a separate cashbook for Payroll Fund- Regular, Payroll Fund-TMC, Payroll Fund-TF and Petty Cash Fund in conformity with Sec. 6.2 of COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer was complied with COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular No. 92-382 as per audit recommendation.
