observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to update and post the transactions and reconcile at least the balances of the general ledger and the cashbook/s in order to arrive to an accurate and reliable account balance at any given period and likewise present a fair and accurate financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer was updated upon examination on October 25, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make representation with DPWH in Ubay, Bohol and disclose the estimated costs of the repair/rehabilitation of the Trinidad Municipal Building in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The LGU made representation with DPWH Ubay who constructed the New Municipal Building to disclose the estimated costs of the Trinidad Municipal Building which was constructed by DPWH. We already prepared a request for a turnover certificate which DPWH Ubay Branch will provide us with the necessary documentation that we could use as basis to record the New Municipal Hall cost in our PPE account and they promised to do so this February 2018.
observations.reason: Except for the recognition of the new municipal hall which was constructed by DPWH; awaiting DPWH documentation for turnover.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management immediately prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) involving the two parties, and identify a recipient People's Organization (PO) for the second unit of Flat Bed Dryer so that the asset may be used for its intended beneficiary.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) involving the two parties. Meanwhile, the floating tillers were already distributed to the barangays. The second unit of Flat Bed Dryer was already installed for it to be ready and fit.
observations.reason: No MOA was made in connection with the second unit of Flat Bed Dryer; floating tillers were distributed and second unit installed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management monitor and follow up the extent of the physical inventory conducted by the Inventory Committee in order to ensure that the inventories and Report of Physical Count of property, plant and Inventories (RPCI) and the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared, consolidated and submitted in to the Auditor within the reglementary period as required under Sec. 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: Management led by the General Services Office (GSO) conducted and submitted the consolidated Report of Physical Count of Inventories and the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to regularly update the posting of transactions and reconcile at least the balances of the general ledger and the cashbook/s for all funds of the Municipal Treasurer so as to present reliable accounting records and financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer complied with COA Circular 97-002 and COA Circular No. 92-382 as recommended. During the cash examination on October 25, 2017, it was noted that the cashbook of the Municipal Treasurer was updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to exert extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending collection bills or demand letters to the concerned persons identified as debtors. Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise request for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were delivered to the concerned debtors but no collections were recovered as of December 31, 2017.
observations.reason: A balance of P150,060.33 still remained in the books which are all past due and not yet recovered as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend the management, being the source agency, to coordinate with the
proper officials of the implementing agency in their subsequent fund transfers to
be able to execute a Memorandum of Agreement in pursuance of COA Circular No.
2012-001.

We recommend that management require the LDRRMO to closely monitor
the utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to
ensure that planned program/projects/activities are duly implemented. We also
recommend the management to submit an explanation for incurring expenses in
excess of the amount allowed by law for LDRRMF. Lastly, we recommend that
management faithfully adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2012-002 dated
September 12, 2012 to facilitate timely review of the required LDRMM report.

We recommend that management plan the Local School Board Budget for
Special Education Fund (SEF) ahead of the deadline set in RA 5447.

We recommend the disbursing officer to strictly observe and comply with
established laws, rules and regulations on documentary and other requirements in
the disbursement of public funds, pursuant to Section 4 (5) & (6) of P.D. 1445,
among other pertinent provisions, to prevent loss of government funds. We further
recommend that if in some cases wherein the Municipal Mayor is temporarily
absent or on official travel, management must assign an official next in rank who is
familiar with the payroll and petty cash transactions (especially that the nature is
recurring) to facilitate the approval of such transactions in lieu of the Agency
Head’s approval per Sec. 344 of RA 7160 which provides that:
“In case of temporary absence or incapacity of the department head or chief of
office, the officer next-in-rank shall automatically perform his function and he shall be
fully responsible therefore.”

We recommend the Agency Head to issue an appointment to the Municipal
Treasurer designating her as an alternate/substitute disbursing officer for the
payroll fund of LGU and attach copy to the cash advance voucher in consonance
with Sec. 4.2.2 of COA Circular 97-002, dated February 10, 1997.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and record succeeding
transactions in a separate cashbook for Payroll Fund-Regular, Payroll Fund-TMC,
Payroll Fund-TF and Petty Cash Fund in conformity with Sec. 6.2 of COA Circular
97-002, dated February 10, 1997 and Sec. 48 (q) of COA Circular No. 92-382, dated
July 3, 1992.

We recommend the Municipal Treasurer to update the posting of all
transaction, including adjustments/correcting entries, in the cashbook.

We recommend that management make representation with DPWH in Ubay,
Bohol and the other implementing agencies for the turn-over of the buildings and
other records. We also recommend that management disclose the estimated costs of
the repair/rehabilitation of the Trinidad Municipal Building in the Notes to
Financial Statements.

We recommend that management monitor and follow up the extent of the physical inventory Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared, consolidated and submitted in to the Auditor within the reglementary period as required under Sec. 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Implemented and RPCI for CY 2015 and 2016 were not completed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to perform immediate review/checking of unaccounted forms and require the concerned accountable officers to submit the respective results within (5) working days from the receipt hereof. We also recommend the Municipal Treasurer to periodically monitor the inflow and outflow of accountable forms through proper maintenance of up-to-date Consolidated Report of Accountable Forms (CRAAF) derived from various accountable officer’s individual RAAFs and see to it that they are thoroughly reviewed and verified pursuant Sec. 30, Chapter 3 of LGU NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Unaccounted accountable forms without money value were verified through submission of RCDs to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the submission of the liquidation reports of all financial assistance released in CY 2015 in accordance with the pertinent documentary requirements enumerated under COA Circular No’s 2004-008 and 2007-001. Henceforth, financial assistance to barangays and NGOs/POs shall be duly liquidated and accounted for in the books of accounts of the LGU in accordance with the same COA Circular in order to afford proper review and evaluation of the utilization of the fund released. Likewise instruct the Municipal Accountant to record future fund releases as debit to Due from Local Government Units (not yet liquidated during the year P xxx) DR - Due from Non-Government Organizations/People’s Organizations (not yet liquidated during the year) CR - Prior Period Adjustments
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Most of the fund transfers granted during the year were liquidated as of year-end, thus, were not reverted back to its respective receivable accounts. The residual unliquidated financial aids were already adjusted back to its receivable accounts as of December 31, 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the above mentioned checks and the Municipal Accountant to set up the cash and payable accounts relative to these transactions to conform with Section 59 of the NGAS Manual Volume I. Likewise, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to make a proper review of the list of outstanding checks and regularly prepare the journal entries for the cancellation of stale checks.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Adjustments were made in the books by the Municipal accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Ms. Henrickson be issued immediately with an office order designating her as Disbursing Officer also for the regular petty cash fund and payroll accounts of all the funds – GF, SEF and TF. We also recommend the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to review the bonding of Ms. Henrickson upon the issuance of the subsequent office order to achieve proper bonding.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Ms. Henrickson was issued immediately with an office order designating her as Disbursing Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer regularly update the posting of transactions and reconcile at least quarterly the balances of the general ledger and the cashbook/s in order to arrive to an accurate and reliable account balance at any given period and likewise present a fair and accurate financial statement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 It was explained by the Municipal Treasurer that the balances were carried out as reflected in the cashbook. The corresponding adjustments are still to be traced by the accounting and treasurer’s office.

observations.recommendation: Should ensure that liquidation of the financial assistance granted to NGOs/POs be made upon completion of the project or the intended purpose has been served in accordance with existing regulations and to refrain from extending grant to recipients with unliquidated financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The Municipal Treasurer has exerted extra efforts to collect these receivables by sending demand letters and even delivered some of the letters personally to the concerned persons. Some collections were made as of CY 2014.

observations.recommendation: Devise effective measures to ensure collections of receivables, otherwise, if proved futile, request for the write-off of those accounts determined to be uncollectible from the Commission on Audit pursuant to Section 36 of PD 1445 so as not to overstate the assets of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The Municipal Treasurer has sent demand letters and even delivered some personally of the concerned persons. Some collections were made as of CY 2014.
observations.reason: A balance of P150,060.83 still remained in the books aged above 3 years.
